tasks.description: Add the shared interaction policy model and provider/context wiring: create the `mobile-interaction-model` helper; extend `useTimelineState.ts`, `useTimelineState.types.ts`, `useTimelineState.contexts.ts`, `TimelineEditorContext.tsx`, and `VideoEditorProvider.tsx`/`VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx` so `deviceClass`, per-event `inputModality`, `interactionMode`, `gestureOwner`, `precisionEnabled`, `contextTarget`, and `inspectorTarget` flow through `useTimelineEditorData()` / `useTimelineEditorOps()` without bypassing the provider, while keeping `interactionStateRef` as the only save/poll gate.
tasks.executor_notes: Added `mobile-interaction-model` and threaded `deviceClass`, `inputModality`, `interactionMode`, `gestureOwner`, `precisionEnabled`, `contextTarget`, and `inspectorTarget` through `useTimelineState()` into the split editor data/ops contexts and `VideoEditorProvider`. Provider coverage now proves consumers can read and update the policy through `useTimelineEditorData()` / `useTimelineEditorOps()` without bypassing the provider. Verified with `npm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false` and `npm exec vitest run src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx`. `interactionStateRef` remains the only save/poll gate because `useTimelineSave()` still receives only that ref and no second persistence suppression path was added.

tasks.description: Make shell and inspector affordances policy-driven in `VideoEditorShell.tsx`, `PropertiesPanel.tsx`, and `ClipPanel.tsx`: add the phone mode bar (`Browse`, `Select`, `Move`, `Trim`, `Precision`), make tablet properties access selection-aware and faster than the current preview/properties toggle alone, and expose inspector-first alternatives for trim, move, move-to-track, split, delete, mute, and clip actions so common phone edits never require fragile direct manipulation.
tasks.executor_notes: Made shell and inspector affordances policy-driven without introducing a second mobile editor path. `VideoEditorShell` now reads the shared interaction policy, renders a persistent phone mode bar for `Browse`/`Select`/`Move`/`Trim` plus a `Precision` toggle, keeps the inspector target in sync with selection, and surfaces a selection-aware inspector affordance in condensed tablet layouts that can jump directly to properties. `PropertiesPanel` and `ClipPanel` now provide explicit inspector-first controls for trim, move, move-to-track via track up/down, split, mute, delete, and precision so common touch edits do not depend on fragile direct manipulation. Verified with `npm exec vitest run src/tools/video-editor/components/__tests__/PreviewPersistence.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx` and `npm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`.

tasks.description: Implement modality-aware timeline arbitration in `TimeRuler.tsx`, `TimelineEditor.tsx`, `TimelineCanvas.tsx`, `useMarqueeSelect.ts`, and `useClipDrag.ts`: resolve gesture ownership from device class plus `PointerEvent.pointerType`, keep the ruler as the dedicated scrub zone, make empty-space touch drag pan by default, gate `touch-action`/`preventDefault`/pointer capture until ownership is granted, enlarge touch trim hit zones, and preserve grouped drag plus deferred-save behavior by continuing to set `interactionStateRef.current.drag/resize` only when editing actually starts.
tasks.executor_notes: Implemented modality-aware timeline arbitration without introducing a second mobile timeline or a second persistence gate. `TimelineEditor` now passes `deviceClass`, `inputModality`, `interactionMode`, `gestureOwner`, `setInputModalityFromPointerType`, and `setGestureOwner` into `useClipDrag`, `useMarqueeSelect`, `TimeRuler`, and `TimelineCanvas`. `TimeRuler` now claims the dedicated scrub zone through shared gesture ownership. `useMarqueeSelect` keeps empty-space touch drag pan-first unless tablet touch selection mode explicitly allows marquee. `useClipDrag` continues to set `interactionStateRef.current.drag` only after drag threshold. `TimelineCanvas` now expands touch trim handles in trim mode and defers resize pointer capture, `preventDefault()`, `pendingOpsRef`, and `interactionStateRef.current.resize` until the resize gesture crosses threshold, preserving `interactionStateRef` as the only save/poll deferral gate. Verified with `npm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false` and `npm exec vitest run src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.test.tsx`.

tasks.description: Apply the selection, move, context, and precision contract in `useMultiSelect.ts`, `useTimelineSelection.ts`, `useClipDrag.ts`, `ClipAction.tsx`, `TimelineCanvas.tsx`, `ShotGroupContextMenu.tsx`, and `useKeyboardShortcuts.ts`: implement the phone-vs-iPad selection and move rules, visible overflow alternatives to long-press, frame-based precision nudges, and preserved desktop/hardware-keyboard semantics; fold in the open shot-group touch gap so navigation, unpin/delete, and final-video switching are reachable without hover or `onContextMenu`-only affordances.
tasks.executor_notes: Applied the selection, move, context, shot-group, and precision contract through the existing provider-backed editor stack. Added touch-selection helpers to the shared interaction model, taught `useClipDrag` to treat touch `Select` taps as toggle selection and touch `Move` taps as selection-preserving when a drag never starts, and extended `useMultiSelect` / `useTimelineSelection` with a no-collapse preserve path so additive selections survive touch move attempts. Added visible overflow affordances for clip and shot-group actions on phone/tablet layouts so navigation, unpin/delete, and final-video switching are reachable without hover or `onContextMenu`-only entry points. Added precision-aware keyboard seeking with frame-step `Alt` + arrow behavior gated by precision mode, while leaving desktop and hardware-keyboard defaults unchanged otherwise. Verified with `npm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false` and `npm exec vitest run src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.test.tsx`.

tasks.description: Bring preview overlay editing onto the same interaction contract in `OverlayEditor.tsx`, `PreviewPanel.tsx`, and `VideoEditorShell.tsx`: switch to pointer-driven ownership, keep phone overlay transforms inspector-first, prevent preview interactions from stealing touches intended for the inspector or timeline mode bar, and apply shared accessibility rules across shell, timeline, and preview including 44x44 touch targets, no hover-only actions, screen-reader labels for interaction state, and reduced-motion-safe transitions.
tasks.executor_notes: Brought preview overlay editing onto the same provider-backed interaction contract without adding a second mobile preview path. `PreviewPanel` now resolves preview background interactions through pointer events, publishes preview interaction state for assistive tech, and passes `deviceClass`, `inputModality`, `interactionMode`, `gestureOwner`, and gesture-owner setters into `OverlayEditor`. `OverlayEditor` now uses pointer events plus preview gesture ownership, claims `preview` only for actual overlay transforms, keeps phone touch inspector-first by skipping direct transform starts and hiding touch-direct manipulation controls there, and enlarges touch-visible preview controls to 44x44 targets where direct manipulation remains allowed. `VideoEditorShell` now applies larger touch targets to shell and preview action controls, adds live screen-reader labels for interaction state, and adds reduced-motion-safe transition fallbacks. Verified with `npm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false` and `npm exec vitest run src/tools/video-editor/components/__tests__/PreviewPersistence.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/RemotionPreview.test.tsx`.

tasks.description: Extend the existing automated coverage for the new contract: add pure decision-table assertions in an existing nearby suite, expand `VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx`, `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`, `useClipDrag.test.tsx`, `ClipAction.test.tsx`, `PreviewPersistence.test.tsx`, and `useTimelinePersistence.test.tsx` to cover provider publication, touch pan-vs-edit ownership, trim-handle gating, long-press move arming, tablet touch-vs-mouse splits, shot-group touch entry points, and persistence/layout regressions without introducing a second save path.
tasks.executor_notes: Extended the existing editor coverage without adding a second save path or new standalone suites. Added pure interaction decision-table assertions to `VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx`, expanded `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` to verify touch trim-handle gating and reachable touch shot-group actions, expanded `useClipDrag.test.tsx` to cover tablet touch-vs-mouse drag routing and touch move-mode drag arming, expanded `ClipAction.test.tsx` to cover labeled visible overflow entry points for multi-selection, expanded `PreviewPersistence.test.tsx` to lock the phone-mode touch target regression, and expanded `useTimelinePersistence.test.tsx` to verify saves remain deferred until both drag and resize are idle. Verified with `npm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false` and `npm exec vitest run src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/__tests__/PreviewPersistence.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelinePersistence.test.tsx`.

tasks.description: Run the full verification loop as the final task: execute the most relevant existing editor test suites for provider, shell, timeline canvas, clip action, clip drag, preview persistence, and timeline persistence coverage; write a short temporary repro script that exercises device-class plus `pointerType` arbitration and confirms iPad touch behavior does not activate for iPad mouse/trackpad, run it, delete it, then complete manual smoke checks on iPhone Safari, iPad Safari touch, and iPad Safari with hardware keyboard/trackpad. If anything fails, fix the code and rerun until green.
tasks.executor_notes: Ran the final automated verification loop successfully with `npm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false` and `npm exec vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/__tests__/PreviewPersistence.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelinePersistence.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/RemotionPreview.test.tsx`; all 7 modules passed. Wrote a temporary repro script for tablet `deviceClass` plus `pointerType` arbitration, ran it successfully with `node --import tsx .megaplan/plans/create-a-concrete-mobile-and-20260410-0149/tmp-pointer-arbitration-repro.ts`, and removed the script afterward. The Vitest run still shows the existing non-failing React `act(...)` warning in the ClipAction context-menu path. The task remains incomplete only because the required manual Safari smoke checks on iPhone Safari, iPad Safari touch, and iPad Safari with hardware keyboard/trackpad could not be performed in this terminal-only environment.

watch_items: Do not fork a second mobile timeline; extend the existing editor shell, timeline, preview, and provider stack.
Thread the interaction policy through `VideoEditorProvider` and the split editor contexts; downstream consumers should not bypass the provider by reading `useTimelineState()` directly.
Resolve behavior from baseline device class plus per-event `PointerEvent.pointerType`; iPad touch rules must never leak onto iPad mouse/trackpad or pen interactions.
`interactionStateRef` remains the sole save/poll deferral gate for drag and resize; do not add a second persistence suppression path.
Phone phase 1 stays inspector-first for complex edits, overlay transforms, and track reassignment; direct manipulation is additive, not mandatory.
Empty-space touch drag should pan by default; marquee or edit gestures only win when the current mode and gesture owner explicitly allow them.
Trim starts only from explicit handles or `Trim` mode, with touch-expanded hit zones activated only after ownership is granted.
Desktop and hardware-keyboard flows must remain intact, including current pointer semantics and `useKeyboardShortcuts()` behavior on iPad with keyboard/trackpad.
Address open FLAG-003 during execution: shot-group navigation, unpin/delete, and final-video switching need visible, non-hover, non-`onContextMenu` touch entry points and 44x44 targets.
Preview interactions must not steal touches intended for the inspector sheet, timeline mode bar, or timeline canvas.
Apply shared accessibility rules across shell, timeline, and preview: no hover-only affordances, screen-reader state labels, and reduced-motion-safe transitions.

Use existing phone/tablet buckets from `src/shared/hooks/mobile/index.ts` as the device-class signal; do not introduce new responsive breakpoint logic unless implementation proves the current buckets insufficient.
Manual smoke validation must cover iPhone Safari, iPad Safari touch, and iPad Safari with hardware keyboard/trackpad on the same build.

sense_checks.question: Can shell, timeline, preview, and properties consumers read and update the new interaction policy through `useTimelineEditorData()` / `useTimelineEditorOps()` without calling `useTimelineState()` directly, and does `interactionStateRef` remain the only persistence gate?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. The interaction policy is now published on `useTimelineEditorData()` and updated through `useTimelineEditorOps()` via `VideoEditorProvider`, so shell, timeline, preview, and properties consumers do not need to call `useTimelineState()` directly. `interactionStateRef` is still the sole save/poll gate because the persistence path still only passes that ref into `useTimelineSave()` and the new policy state does not participate in save suppression.

sense_checks.question: On phone and tablet layouts, are explicit mode controls and inspector fallbacks visible enough that common edits do not require fragile direct touch manipulation?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. Phone layouts now expose a persistent mode bar and inspector button that both route through the shared interaction policy, while touch-oriented inspector action groups in `PropertiesPanel` and `ClipPanel` make trim, move, track moves, split, mute, delete, and precision visible without relying on direct timeline manipulation. Condensed tablet layouts also surface a selection-aware `Clip`/`Selection` inspector affordance and auto-open the properties side when a selection is present, so access is faster than relying on the preview/properties toggle alone.

sense_checks.question: Do ruler scrub, empty-canvas pan or marquee, clip drag, and trim claim ownership correctly by `pointerType` and device class, with iPad mouse or trackpad still following desktop behavior?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. Ruler scrub, empty-canvas marquee, clip drag, and trim now resolve ownership through the shared interaction policy using both baseline device class and per-event `pointerType`. `TimeRuler` owns scrub gestures as the dedicated ruler zone, touch empty-space interactions stay pan-first unless tablet touch select mode explicitly allows marquee, touch clip drag only arms in move mode, and trim claims `trim` ownership only after the resize threshold is crossed. Because touch-specific gating is resolved from `pointerType`, iPad mouse and trackpad still follow desktop behavior instead of inheriting iPad touch rules.

sense_checks.question: Do phone and iPad selection, move, context, shot-group, precision, and keyboard rules match the contract, with visible non-hover touch entry points for actions previously gated by right-click or hover?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. Phone and iPad touch selection and move behavior now follow the shared interaction contract instead of desktop-only modifier assumptions: touch `Select` mode toggles clip membership, touch `Move` mode preserves additive selections unless a drag actually begins, and desktop plus hardware-keyboard semantics remain on the existing path because the new branching only activates for touch modality. Clip and shot-group actions that were previously hover- or context-menu-gated now have visible touch triggers, and precision mode adds frame-step arrow-key seeking without replacing the existing desktop shortcut behavior.

sense_checks.question: Does preview overlay editing follow the same interaction contract and accessibility rules without stealing gestures from the mode bar, inspector, or timeline?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. Preview overlay editing now follows the same interaction contract as the timeline: overlay transforms start from pointer events, claim shared `gestureOwner` as `preview`, and release that ownership when the interaction ends. Phone touch remains inspector-first because overlay transform handles and preview-side transform buttons are hidden there and touch pointer-down only selects and routes focus back to inspector state, while tablet touch and mouse/trackpad paths keep direct manipulation. `PreviewPanel` and `VideoEditorShell` also add live interaction-state labels, larger touch targets, and reduced-motion-safe transitions so preview controls stay accessible without stealing touches from the mode bar, inspector, or timeline.

sense_checks.question: Do the updated automated tests cover provider wiring, decision-table routing, timeline arbitration, touch trim gating, shot-group touch actions, and deferred-save regressions using the existing test infrastructure?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. The updated automated coverage stays within the existing test infrastructure and now exercises the shared interaction contract across provider publication, pure routing decisions, touch trim gating, tablet touch-vs-mouse drag behavior, visible touch shot-group and clip action entry points, and deferred-save behavior. The persistence coverage still runs through the existing `interactionStateRef` gate and explicitly proves there is no second save path by keeping save deferred until both drag and resize are idle.

sense_checks.question: Have the targeted suites, temporary repro script, and manual Safari device checks all passed after reruns, with the repro script removed from the repo?
sense_checks.executor_note: No. The targeted suites passed after reruns, the temporary pointer-arbitration repro passed, and the repro script was removed from the repo, but the required manual Safari smoke checks on iPhone Safari, iPad Safari touch, and iPad Safari with hardware keyboard/trackpad could not be performed from this terminal-only environment.

meta_commentary: Execute in the approved order: land the policy and context plumbing first, then shell and inspector affordances, then timeline gesture ownership, then higher-risk selection or move or trim behavior, and only after that convert preview overlay interactions. Keep the implementation centered on one shared interaction policy and one persistence gate; if a shortcut seems to require a mobile-only codepath or a second save or sync guard, stop and reroute through the existing provider/context surface and `interactionStateRef`. The one intentional refinement beyond the approved steps is to absorb FLAG-003 into the context-action work so touch users gain reliable shot-group actions while the gesture contract is being changed. Because this finalize brief forbids new standalone test files, satisfy the plan’s pure-policy coverage by extending existing nearby suites unless the repository already has an accepted colocated pattern that does not increase test-surface sprawl.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Add provider-backed interaction policy and modality resolver through the shared editor contexts
Make shell and inspector affordances policy-driven for phone and tablet editing
Route scrub, pan, marquee, drag, and trim by device class plus input modality
Define selection, move, context, precision, and keyboard behavior across touch and desktop inputs
Bring preview overlay editing and accessibility onto the same interaction contract
Validate the policy with automated coverage, a temporary repro harness, and manual device checks

validation.completeness_notes: All six approved plan steps are mapped. Step 6’s testing intent is preserved, but the file-placement detail is adapted to the finalize requirement that forbids introducing new standalone test files; pure-policy assertions should be folded into existing suites. T4 intentionally absorbs the still-open shot-group touch concern from FLAG-003 so that critique feedback remains visible during execution rather than being deferred out of the interaction pass.
